---
title: "Pareto SAT Example"
author: "Tyler Finnerty"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = normalizePath(".."))

library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(readxl)
```

```{r}
sat_data <- read_xlsx("data/downloaded/pareto_frontier_example.xlsx")

ggplot(sat_data, aes(x = SAT, y = 0, shape = Race)) + 
    geom_point(size = 5) + 
    scale_shape_manual(values = c("Circle" = 1, "Square" = 0)) + 
    theme(panel.background = element_blank(),
          axis.text.y = element_blank(),
          axis.ticks = element_blank()) +
    labs(y = "", x = "SAT Score") + 
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0)
```

```{r}
data <- sat_data

# Sort the data by SAT score in descending order
data <- data %>% arrange(desc(SAT))

# Initialize vectors to store error and unfairness values
error <- numeric(nrow(data))
unfairness <- numeric(nrow(data))

# Initialize variables to store counts of successes by race
success_circle <- sum(data$Success[data$Race == "Circle"])
success_square <- sum(data$Success[data$Race == "Square"])

# Iterate through each subset of individuals with decreasing SAT scores
for (i in 1:nrow(data)) {
  subset_data <- data[1:i, ]
  
  # Calculate error: sum of 0s in accepted set and 1s in rejected set
  error[i] <- sum(subset_data$Success == 0) + 
      (sum(data$Success == 1) - sum(subset_data$Success == 1))
  
  # Calculate unfairness: absolute difference in success counts between Circle and Square races
  unfairness[i] <- (
      (sum(data$Success[data$Race == "Square"] == 1) - 
      sum(subset_data$Success[subset_data$Race == "Square"] == 1)) -
      (sum(data$Success[data$Race == "Circle"] == 1) - 
      sum(subset_data$Success[subset_data$Race == "Circle"] == 1)))
}

# Create a data frame with error and unfairness values
pareto_data <- data.frame(Error = error, Unfairness = unfairness)

# Plot the Pareto frontier
ggplot(pareto_data, aes(x = Error, y = Unfairness)) +
    geom_point(size = 4) +
    labs(x = "Error", y = "Unfairness") +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, max(pareto_data$Error), by = 2)) +
    theme_minimal()

```
